articles.title: Multibagger small cap stock Standard Engineering share price to be in focus on Monday; here's why
articles.summary: SETL's acquisition of a controlling stake in GScale Energy marks its entry into the AI data centre market. With a strategic investment of ₹500 crore, SETL seeks to leverage GScale's expertise for growth, targeting 40-50% increase in core revenue by FY2027.
articles.content:
Shares of multibagger small-cap stock Standard Engineering Technology Ltd. (SETL) are likely to remain in focus on Monday, June 30, after the company announced its entry into the artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure space through the acquisition of a controlling stake in GScale Energy Pvt. Ltd.

The company's board has approved the acquisition of up to a 51% equity stake in GScale Energy, a company specialising in data centre engineering and infrastructure solutions. Once the transaction is completed, GScale will operate as a subsidiary of SETL.

Instead of building AI data centre capabilities from scratch, SETL said the acquisition will provide immediate access to GScale's engineering expertise, relationships with hyperscale customers and existing letters of intent (LOIs). According to the company, the move positions it to tap into the rapidly expanding AI data centre market, where global capital expenditure is estimated at US$5.2-6.7 trillion by 2030.

The acquisition will be executed through a mix of fresh capital infusion and a strategic share swap with GScale's existing shareholders. SETL has earmarked around ₹190 crore for the first phase of the transaction.

As part of a broader expansion strategy, the company has also approved an overall investment of nearly ₹500 crore, which will be used for equity acquisition, business expansion and working capital requirements. The company said the entire investment will be funded through internal accruals.

While GScale will continue to be managed by its existing founder and leadership team, SETL will provide strategic direction, financial backing and manufacturing capabilities to support future growth.

Alongside its AI expansion, SETL remains optimistic about its existing engineering operations.

"Looking ahead to FY2027, we remain confident in the underlying strength of our core engineering business. Based on our current order pipeline, customer engagements, and ongoing growth initiatives, management is targeting approximately 40–50% revenue growth in our existing operations, subject to prevailing market conditions and execution outcomes," the company said in its exchange filing.

The company believes the acquisition will also strengthen its position in India's fast-growing AI data centre ecosystem by combining GScale's concept-to-commissioning capabilities with SETL's engineering and execution expertise. It noted that India could require around US$20-25 billion of investments in AI and hyperscale data centre infrastructure by 2030 and aims to emerge as a key engineering partner in this segment.

The announcement comes after a sharp recovery in SETL's share price.

After falling to a record low of ₹104.56 in March, the small-cap stock staged a strong turnaround, rising around 30% in April as buying interest returned. The momentum accelerated further in June, with the stock gaining more than 60% so far this month.

Overall, the shares have surged over 100% from their March lows and are up around 42% in 2026, significantly outperforming many peers in the volatile small-cap segment.

Disclaimer: This story is for educational purposes only. Please consult with an investment advisor before making any investment decisions.

Pranati Deva is a seasoned financial journalist with over a decade of experience in high-pressure newsroom environments, currently working as a Senior Sub Editor at LiveMint. Over the years, she has developed a reputation for sharp editorial judgement, a strong grasp of market dynamics, and the ability to translate complex financial developments into clear, engaging stories for a wide audience. <br><br> Her core areas of coverage include stock markets, leading listed companies, currencies, and commodities, with a particular strength in fast-paced, real-time market reporting. She is known for handling breaking market news, earnings-driven stock movements, and macroeconomic developments with speed, accuracy, and context—qualities that are essential in financial journalism. <br><br> Pranati has built a diverse and credible professional track record across some of India’s most respected news organisations, including MintGenie, CNBC-TV18, Business Standard and EconomicTimes.com. During her stints at these platforms, she produced data-driven market stories, curated and steered live blogs during volatile trading sessions, and conducted interviews with market veterans, fund managers, economists, and industry experts. Her work often combines on-ground reporting with analytical depth, helping readers make sense of daily market fluctuations and longer-term trends. An alumnus of the Symbiosis Institute of Media and Communications and Hansraj College, University of Delhi, Pranati brings a strong academic foundation to her journalism. She specialises in real-time financial reporting, with a keen focus on precision, balance, and insight, aiming to decode market movements in a way that is both informative and accessible to readers across experience levels.

articles.title: IPO GMPs: CSM Technologies IPO vs Knack Packaging IPO vs Aastha Spintex IPO; here's what GMP hints
articles.summary: India's IPO market remains vibrant with multiple offerings like Knack Packaging and Aastha Spintex. The subscription for CSM Technologies is progressing well, with strong grey market premiums suggesting positive listing outcomes.
articles.content:
India's primary market continues to witness strong momentum, with several companies tapping investors across sectors ranging from technology and textiles to packaging.

While ongoing issues such as CSM Technologies remain open for subscription, a fresh batch of IPOs, including Knack Packaging and Aastha Spintex, is set to hit the market next week, reflecting sustained fundraising activity despite intermittent volatility in the secondary market.

The healthy pipeline also comes as companies continue to receive regulatory approvals, and investor appetite for quality public offerings remains intact.

CSM Technologies IPO GMP today is +4. Considering the upper end of the IPO price band and the current premium in the grey market, the estimated listing price of the CSM Technologies share was ₹117 apiece, which is 3.54% higher than the IPO price of ₹113.

Based on the recent nine sessions of grey market activity, today's IPO GMP shows an upward trend, indicating a likely strong listing. The minimum GMP recorded is ₹0.00, while the maximum GMP is ₹4, as per expert opinions.

The ₹145.78-crore initial public offering (IPO) of IT solutions provider CSM Technologies was subscribed 66% by the second day of bidding on Thursday, 25 June. The issue, priced in the range of ₹107-113 per share, will close on 29 June. Trading remained suspended on Friday, 26 June, due to the Muharram holiday.

The company plans to utilise the proceeds from the fresh issue to fund working capital requirements, repay debt, and support inorganic growth initiatives.

Aastha Spintex IPO GMP today is +4.75. Considering the upper end of the IPO price band and the current premium in the grey market, the estimated listing price of the Aastha Spintex share was ₹140.75 apiece, which is 3.49% higher than the IPO price of ₹136.

Based on grey market activity over the last 17 sessions, today's IPO GMP is trending upward, indicating a robust listing. The minimum GMP recorded is ₹0.00, while the maximum is ₹4.75, according to analysts.

Textile manufacturer Aastha Spintex will open its ₹170-crore IPO on Monday, 29 June. The public issue, priced at ₹125-136 per share, will remain open for subscription until Wednesday, 1 July.

The company intends to utilise the IPO proceeds towards a part payment for the acquisition of Falcon Yarns Pvt. Ltd., fund the acquired entity's working capital requirements through inter-corporate deposits, and meet general corporate purposes.

Knack Packaging IPO GMP today is +11.5. Considering the upper end of the IPO price band and the current premium in the grey market, the estimated listing price of the Knack Packaging share was ₹181.5 apiece, which is 6.76% higher than the IPO price of ₹170.

Packaging solutions provider Knack Packaging will launch its ₹439.5-crore IPO for public subscription on Wednesday, 1 July.

The issue comprises a fresh issue of equity shares worth ₹380 crore and an offer-for-sale (OFS) of up to 35 lakh shares aggregating to ₹59.5 crore by existing shareholders. The price band has been fixed at ₹161-170 per share, valuing the company at around ₹2,080 crore at the upper end.

The public issue will close on Friday, 3 July, while the anchor investor portion will open for bidding on Tuesday, 30 June.

Disclaimer: This story is for educational purposes only. The views and recommendations above are those of individual analysts or broking companies, not Mint. We advise investors to check with certified experts before making any investment decisions.

articles.title: [Closing Market] KOSPI Falls 5.81% to 8,411.21 Amid Won Closes at 1532 Per Dollar
articles.summary: The domestic stock market, which had been riding a sharp upward trend the previous day, staged a panic market in just one day as it took a direct hit from an i
articles.content:
KOSPI Closes with 5.8% Plunge After Circuit Breaker Triggered by 8% Intraday Crash While Won-Dollar Exchange Rate Closes at 1532.0 Won on Verbal Intervention by Foreign Exchange Authorities

The domestic stock market, which had been riding a sharp upward trend the previous day, staged a panic market in just one day as it took a direct hit from an inflation shock originating from the US stock market. On June 26, the KOSPI Market closed at 8,411.21, crashing by a staggering 519.09 points (5.81%) from the previous trading day.

The market’s steep decline on this day originated from anticipated macroeconomic bad news. Overnight, the May Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) price index announced by the US Department of Commerce rose by 4.1% compared to the same month of the previous year, recording the highest level in 3 years and 1 month since April 2023. Consequently, the risk of the US ‘Higher for longer’ interest rates and a benchmark interest rate hike within the year directly struck the capital market.

Opening simultaneously with a drop of 117.12 points (1.31%) to 8,813.18, the index terrifyingly expanded its decline amidst the pressure of foreign capital outflow that spread across the entire Asian stock market. Particularly into the afternoon, as program selling by foreigners and institutions poured out concurrently, the index was briefly pushed down to 8,126.84, plummeting by more than 8.9% intraday.

In this process, a ‘Circuit Breaker’, which temporarily halts all stock trading for 20 minutes to mitigate the market shock caused by the plunge in stock prices, was triggered. Driven by retail investors’ bargain hunting and intervention signals from the foreign exchange authorities right before the market close, the index recovered some of its losses, but it was recorded as a cruel Friday where tens of trillions of won evaporated in a single day.

The KOSDAQ index was also powerless in the face of indiscriminate profit-taking selling by foreigners. After starting at 878.04, down 9.77 points (1.10%) from the previous trading day, the KOSDAQ received continuous downward pressure throughout the trading session and failed to escape the bearish trend. Selling that bordered on dumping emerged, centered around theme stocks and small-to-mid-sized IT component stocks that had recently led the KOSDAQ market, threatening the psychological support line of investors.

Indiscriminate Bomb of Selling by Foreigners and Institutions Vs. Individuals Building A Defense Line Alone

The movements by supply and demand entities on this day can be summarized as a steep confrontation between the escape of terrified institutions and foreigners, and the retail investors who absorbed this alone. Foreigners and institutions net sold 5.4282 trillion won (about $3.54 billion) and 4.4473 trillion won, respectively. Retail investors alone net bought 9.5172 trillion won.

Foreign investors spearheaded the crashing market by pouring out record net selling in the KOSPI Market. This is interpreted as them engaging in Asset Allocation, sharply reducing the proportion of emerging market assets in line with the rebound in US Treasury yields and the trend of a strong dollar. Taking massive short positions not only in the spot market but also in the futures market, they acted as the fuse for the index’s decline.

Institutional investors, centered around financial investments, investment trusts, and pension funds, joined the panic selling of foreigners. While they seemed to maintain a wait-and-see attitude in the early trading session, as the index broke away from major support lines and the risk of a Circuit Breaker triggering escalated, they poured out stop-loss volumes, further expanding the decline.

On the other hand, retail investors alone recorded a formidable trillion-won scale ‘storm of net buying’, taking it upon themselves to act as the market’s safety valve. Even in the extreme panic phase where the index collapsed to the 8,100 mark, they brought in an unprecedented level of bargain hunting, building a defense wall so that the index could barely manage to close at the 8,400 mark. However, because the selling pressure from foreigners and institutions was so overwhelming, it was inadequate to reverse the sharp plunge of the index itself.

Top Market Capitalization Stocks Including Semiconductors, Automobiles, and Batteries ‘Sink’ in Unison

Top market capitalization large-cap stocks were utterly devastated. With the deterioration of the macro environment overlapping with the fatigue from short-term rapid surges, the stock prices of the flagship companies that had been leading the domestic stock market staged a massive steep decline.

The semiconductor ‘two tops,’ which were the pillars of the domestic stock market, stood at the center of the index crash. The momentum from US Micron’s strong earnings was completely buried by the US PCE shock. Samsung Electronics closed with a significant drop as concerns over slowing demand for IT sets due to prolonged high interest rates were re-highlighted, and SK hynix, which had recently been rallying on AI favorable factors, also underwent a steep correction as profit-taking selling was intensively dropped. SK Square (-0.43%), which represented the equity value of SK hynix, also could not avoid the chain of crashing trends. The semiconductor sector, including Hanmi Semiconductor (-5.68%), also showed a sharp decline due to concerns over slowing memory demand caused by cost burdens and a retracement from the previous day’s surge.

On this day, Samsung Electronics ended trading at 339,500 won, down 19,000 won (5.30%). Marking a downward trend for the first time in 3 trading days, it expanded its decline to more than 10% intraday. SK Hynix closed at 2,673,000 won ($1,744.78), plunging by 244,000 won (8.36%). Intraday, it dropped by more than 12%.

Hyundai Motor (-4.76%) and Kia, which take a hit from shrinking installment financing if the high interest rate stance is prolonged, plummeted together, adding burden to the index. Large-cap battery stocks such as LG Energy Solution (-6.25%) and Samsung SDI, which had ongoing concerns about the slowdown in the electric vehicle industry (chasm), also became targets of foreigners’ panic selling and expanded their extent of decline.

Samsung Biologics (-3.61%) and Celltrion, the leading stocks in pharmaceuticals and biotechnology, as well as Samsung Electro-Mechanics, which had been withstanding the upward trend alone during the morning on the momentum of automotive electronics and substrates, ultimately reversed to a decline and closed the market as the entire market collapsed in the afternoon. Samsung C&T (-4.72%), which was classified as a safe asset and defensive stock, also received limited downward pressure amidst the vortex of the index’s steep plunge. Among the top stocks, Samsung Life Insurance (-3.36%) and HD Hyundai Heavy Industries (-3.09%) also fell. Samsung Electro-Mechanics closed at the same price as the previous day.

The KOSDAQ closed at 851.37, down 36.44 points (4.10%) from the previous day. In the KOSDAQ, by trading entity, foreigners and institutions net bought 374.1 billion won and 308.1 billion won, respectively, while retail investors alone net sold 692 billion won.

Among the top 10 KOSDAQ stocks, ALTEOGEN (-8.40%), EcoPro BM (-7.15%), EcoPro (-6.47%), Rainbow Robotics (-6.98%), Kolon TissueGene (-4.99%), JUSUNG Engineering (-0.78%), LEENO Industrial (-4.96%), and HLB (-2.65%) declined. Wonik IPS (5.88%) and EO Technics (1.68%) rose.

Won-Dollar Exchange Rate Starts with a Sharp Surge to the 1,540 Won Range, Then Closes at 1,532.0 Won on Authority Intervention

Amidst the domestic regular stock market being engulfed in extreme panic, the Seoul foreign exchange market also showed a roller-coaster move. On this day, the won-dollar exchange rate began trading at 1,547.3 won, up 4.6 won from the previous trading day’s daytime trading closing price (1,542.7 won).

This was the aftermath of the global dollar instantly receiving upward pressure as the US May PCE price index recorded an inflation rate exceeding market expectations, and bets on the won’s weakness increasing in the offshore market. As the stock market crash and large-scale stock selling by foreigners in the early trading session stimulated demand for dollar exchange, the exchange rate briefly soared to 1,549.0 won intraday, keeping the breakthrough of the 1,550 won line right before its eyes.

However, as the exchange rate surged excessively and aggravated macro instability, the atmosphere reversed as foreign exchange authorities heavily released estimated volumes for fine-tuning (smoothing operation) and verbal intervention into the market. Into the afternoon, as the global dollar index slightly reversed to the 101.36 line and the authorities’ strong supply of dollar selling flowed in, the exchange rate gave back all of its gains and instead closed the market at 1,532.0 won, down 10.7 won from the previous day. Although the exchange rate took a breather by closing lower, it clearly revealed that the overall stamina of the financial market had weakened, with the intraday fluctuation range reaching 11.0 won.

Experts in the financial investment industry are analyzing this KOSPI crash as a ‘temporary seizure’ and a ‘transitional growing pain’ that erupted in the process of macro uncertainty maximizing ahead of the upcoming second-quarter corporate earnings season. They point out that while it is true that the deterioration of inflation indicators put the brakes on the Federal Reserve’s monetary policy stance for the second half of the year, the profit fundamentals of the companies themselves have not been damaged.

An official from an asset management company advised, “Supply and demand became tangled as US exceptionalism and inflation concerns overlapped, but narrowing the decline after the triggering of the Circuit Breaker intraday is evidence showing the soundness of the standby buying force.” They added, “While preparing for a volatile market by confirming exchange rate volatility and foreign supply and demand trends for the time being, it is a time that requires an installment buying approach utilizing the oversold sections of leading sectors with solid earnings fundamentals, such as semiconductors and automobiles, along with thorough portfolio risk management.”

articles.title: HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank remain top bets as valuations stay attractive, says Kotak
articles.summary: Kotak Institutional Equities supports leading private banks like HDFC and ICICI, believing they will benefit from improved loan yields and lower funding costs. They remain cautious on loan demand but expect strong asset quality and less pressure on corporate balance sheets.
articles.content:
Frontline private banks are well positioned to outperform as attractive valuations, resilient asset quality, improving funding dynamics, and the potential for earnings-led re-rating create a favourable investment case, according to Kotak Institutional Equities.

The brokerage has retained an overweight stance on leading private lenders, preferring HDFC Bank and ICICI Bank, while State Bank of India (SBI) remains its top pick among public sector banks. It believes the sector is entering a phase in which valuation re-rating will be supported by higher loan yields, lower funding costs, and steady earnings growth rather than rapid credit expansion.

Kotak said two developments could act as catalysts for banking stocks. The first is public sector banks' efforts to improve loan yields, which could provide pricing support across the industry. The second is banks' ability to raise low-cost foreign currency non-resident (FCNR) deposits and other overseas borrowings, helping ease pressure on funding costs.

The brokerage, however, remains cautious on the outlook for loan demand. It expects strong foreign capital inflows to reduce banks' dependence on domestic deposits rather than trigger a meaningful acceleration in credit growth. Recent Reserve Bank of India data also suggests that credit growth has been supported more by substitution from bond markets than by a broad-based increase in borrowing demand.

Kotak expects asset quality to remain strong through FY27, with both private and public sector banks likely to report lower slippages. While lenders may increase provisions ahead of the transition to the Expected Credit Loss (ECL) framework, the brokerage does not expect this to materially weaken the sector.

Retail asset quality is entering this phase from a much stronger position than in FY23, supported by tighter underwriting standards since FY24 and the seasoning of weaker loan cohorts. Unsecured retail loans are expected to witness the sharpest improvement after the stress seen in recent quarters.

Although MSME portfolios could face some pressure if economic growth slows significantly, Kotak believes systemic risks remain limited. Government-backed guarantee schemes such as the Emergency Credit Line Guarantee Scheme (ECLGS) and the Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE) continue to provide meaningful support. Meanwhile, corporate balance sheets remain healthy, enabling banks to comfortably fund credit requirements despite sector-specific uncertainties.

Despite the banking sector's recent outperformance, Kotak believes valuations remain attractive, with most frontline banks still trading below their estimated fair values. The brokerage sees scope for further multiple expansion alongside steady earnings compounding.

Within the private banking space, Kotak continues to favour HDFC Bank and ICICI Bank at current valuations. It believes Axis Bank will need to demonstrate stronger franchise performance before it can command a valuation premium over HDFC Bank.

Among public sector lenders, SBI remains Kotak's preferred pick. While benign asset-quality trends and an improving funding environment could support greater risk-taking, the brokerage believes current valuations already capture much of the potential upside.

Disclaimer: This story is for educational purposes only. The views and recommendations above are those of individual analysts or broking companies, not Mint. We advise investors to check with certified experts before making any investment decisions.

articles.summary: This article provides an overview of COPT Defense Properties (CDP) financial actuals and estimates, including income statement, balance sheet, and cash flow data. It also covers analyst forecasts, stock performance metrics, dividend information, and upcoming earnings dates for...
articles.content:
Frequently asked questions

﻿

Depending on the exchange, the stock ticker may vary. For instance, on BOATS exchange COPT Defense Properties stocks are traded under the ticker CDP.

We've gathered analysts' opinions on COPT Defense Properties future price: according to them, CDP price has a max estimate of 39.00 USD and a min estimate of 34.00 USD. Watch CDP chart and read a more detailed COPT Defense Properties stock forecast: see what analysts think of COPT Defense Properties and suggest that you do with its stocks.

CDP earnings for the last quarter are 0.34 USD per share, whereas the estimation was 0.33 USD resulting in a 2.81% surprise. The estimated earnings for the next quarter are 0.33 USD per share. See more details about COPT Defense Properties earnings.

CDP net income for the last quarter is ‪38.56 M‬ USD, while the quarter before that showed ‪37.50 M‬ USD of net income which accounts for 0.00% change. Track more COPT Defense Properties financial stats to get the full picture.

Yes, CDP dividends are paid quarterly. The last dividend per share was 0.32 USD. As of today, Dividend Yield (TTM)% is 3.51%. Tracking COPT Defense Properties dividends might help you take more informed decisions.

COPT Defense Properties dividend yield was 4.39% in 2025, and payout ratio reached 91.01%. The year before the numbers were 3.81% and 96.19% correspondingly. See high-dividend stocks and find more opportunities for your portfolio.
